vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or dormant. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct healthy specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable biological presence, though it is in a state of severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil/debris. No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot shows no evidence of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows progressive chlorosis and structural collapse. The white rabbit anchor remains, but the plant is failing.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or compacted.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in P3; electronic component (IC chip) on desk surface near P3.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None explicitly identified, but soil darkness suggests potential anaerobic conditions.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-3: Initial state shows P3 with a single leaf and stem. P1 and P2 pots are empty.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-1: Progressive loss of turgor pressure in P3. The stem is drooping significantly.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Total loss of visibility due to lighting failure or extreme plant collapse. The environment has transitioned to near-total darkness.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The Pothos (P3) is likely suffering from root rot or severe light starvation, leading to the observed postural collapse. The absence of P1, P2, and P4 suggests either total mortality or complete burial/loss of the specimens.
anomalies: Electronic component (IC chip) on desk surface.
Eggshell fragments used as soil top-dressing in P3.
Severe lighting degradation in final frames.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment has undergone a catastrophic decline. Starting from a baseline where only one of four expected plants was clearly visible, the sequence documents the slow senescence and collapse of the remaining Pothos specimen. The presence of non-botanical debris (eggshells, electronics) suggests an experimental or neglected setup. The final images indicate a failure in the monitoring environment itself, rendering the plants nearly invisible.
